title: lupinus austrorientalis var. austrorientalis
intro: Lupinus austrorientalis var. austrorientalis, also known as the Australian lupin or narrow-leaved lupin, is a plant species in the genus Lupinus. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly its seeds and leaves, which contain alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Traditionally, the plant's seeds and leaves are prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusions to utilize its medicinal properties.
